<!-- GENERATED by wxcc-dist/assemble.py from the canonical playbook source — do not hand-edit. -->

### Known CLI Limitations

| Limitation | Workaround |
|------------|------------|
| `wxcc-flow events` cannot list the event-spec catalog — prod exposes no event-spec endpoint | Get event names from an exported flow's `eventFlows` (`wxcc-flow export FLOW_ID`) or `docs/reference/flow-designer-flowir.md` § 6 |
| `patch` on drafts created by `consume-template` → server 500 ("Oops... Something broke...") | Patch only FlowIR-authored flows, or save-draft a clean FlowV2 first |
| `save-draft` of a FlowV2 body (no `diagram` block) makes the server auto-regenerate the diagram and re-type **specific** activity widgets to the raw `activityName` — it is selective, not blanket: `fn-activity`→`fn-activity`, `http-request-v2`→`http-request`, `end`→`action` corrupt, while `play-message`/`queue-contact`/`play-music`/`bridged-transfer` stay `action` and `set-variable`/`enum-gateway`/`terminating-action` stay correct → a corrupted `fn-activity`/`http-request` widget then FAILS to open in the Flow Designer UI with "Failed to load flow — Cannot find factory with type [fn-activity]". Logical graph + `validate` are unaffected (only the widget `type` field is wrong — every other widget field is identical). `wxcc-flow copy` alone does NOT do this (stays UI-loadable), but `wxcc-flow update` (rename/re-describe, v2 draft patch) DOES trigger the identical corruption — see the Renaming row below. ⚠ Baseline against a clean `copy` (fn/HTTP widgets read `type: action`) — a "before" `export-ui` that was itself already save-drafted shows no change and reads as a false non-repro. Verified live 2026-07-23 (re-verified 2nd pass). | **Working fix (verified 2026-07-23):** `save-draft` to a copy to generate the graph → `export-ui` → remap each activity widget's `type` back to the correct value (from an uncorrupted `export-ui`; new action nodes → `action`) → `import-ui` (transports the diagram verbatim, so types survive). Full procedure + the activityName→type map in `docs/reference/flow-designer-flowir.md` § "Working CLI Workaround". Plain `copy` is safe for backups |
| `draft` export AND `copy` MAY drop `fnName` on Function (`fn-activity`) nodes — the drop is inconsistent/per-node (some nodes keep `fnName`, some lose it in the same export; re-verified 2nd pass: 1 of 3 dropped, and a flow whose `fnName`s were already restored+reimported round-trips cleanly with none dropped). When any node's `fnName` is null, `validate`, `save-draft`, and `patch` (import) fail with `Import Failure: fnName not found for activity` (ACTIVITY_NOT_FOUND). `validate-id` on the same LIVE draft does NOT flag it (it resolves the function via `fnVersionConfig.id`) — so the flow runs fine live but round-trips break. `patch` re-imports the WHOLE draft, so it fails even when your delta doesn't touch the function node. Verified live 2026-07-23. | Before `save-draft`/`validate`, re-add `fnName` to every `fn-activity` node (the value = the node label minus its `_xxx` suffix, e.g. `parseStoreUserName_r5z` → `fnName: "parseStoreUserName"`; confirm via a clean `validate`). `patch` cannot be used at all on a flow whose stored `fn-activity` nodes lack `fnName` — use `save-draft` with `fnName` restored, or edit in the Flow Designer UI |
| `all-versions-latest` → 500 org-wide when never-published flows exist (the command prints a warning naming the fallback before every call) | Fall back to paged `wxcc-flow all-versions` |
| `validate` / `validate-id` always validate as FLOW (the v2 validate endpoints take no effective flowType); a valid SUBFLOW fails with ACTIVITY_NOT_FOUND on end-subflow | Known server gap — both commands print a warning banner; treat end-subflow errors on subflows as expected |
| `choices` on a cascading input (e.g. queue-contact `destination`) → 400 without parent context — the old fallback to static values is gone | Pass the parent explicitly: `wxcc-flow choices queue-contact destination --parent-input channelType --parent-value TELEPHONY` |
| `consume-template` with SUBFLOW templates → 404 FLOW_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND (feature-flag `flow-control-draft-templates` gated) | Create subflows via `create --type SUBFLOW` |
| `project` / `connector-list` → 404 on the system-provisioned project (project-controller and connector-controller only see user-created projects) | These work with user-created project IDs; the flows/* endpoints accept the system project fine |
| `traces` / `interactions` need a REAL call routed through the flow (Flow Store cannot place calls) | Route an entry point to the flow, dial it, then query |
| Renaming: the v1 merge-patch endpoint silently ignores name/description (200 but no-op) | `wxcc-flow update` uses the v2 draft patch; new name propagates to flow level on next publish. ⚠ But the v2 draft patch ALSO regenerates and selectively corrupts the UI diagram (`fn-activity`→`fn-activity`, `http-request-v2`→`http-request`, `end`→`action` widget types) exactly like `save-draft` — so a flow renamed via `wxcc-flow update` won't open in the Flow Designer UI until fixed via the export-ui → remap → import-ui workaround (see the `save-draft` corruption row above and `docs/reference/flow-designer-flowir.md` § 11). Verified live 2026-07-23: a clean `copy` exported `{fn-activity: action, http-request-v2: action, end: end}`; a rename-only `wxcc-flow update` then exported `{fn-activity: fn-activity, http-request-v2: http-request, end: action}` |

(Rows verified live 2026-07-12.)
